target.title: feat(agents): generalized native compaction ownership for CLI backends
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `ownsNativeCompaction` capability to `CliBackendPlugin` so backends that manage their own transcript compaction can declare it and OpenClaw defers instead of fighting or hard-failing the turn
- Claude-cli is the first adopter: Claude Code compacts its own transcript internally, so OpenClaw returns a no-op from the compaction path
- Codex's existing native-harness compaction path is unchanged (agentHarnessId match still routes to the harness endpoint)
- Generalizes the partial fix in #87785 (codex-scoped) to a capability any CLI backend can opt into

## Problem

`claude-cli` runs Claude Code, which owns its own transcript and auto-compacts when context fills. OpenClaw does not know this, so the safeguard summarizer fires and fights Claude's compaction, hard-failing the turn.

The current workaround is `contextTokens: 1_000_000` so the safeguard never fires. This PR removes the need for that workaround.

## Behavior proof

Production compaction failure captured from a live claude-cli session (session `39bd8759`, 2026-05-16T21:59:57Z):

```
[assistant] [provider: openclaw, model: delivery-mirror]
Context limit exceeded during compaction. I've reset our conversation
to start fresh - please try again.

To prevent this, increase your compaction buffer by setting
`agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokensFloor` to 20000 or higher
in your config.
```

This is the exact failure mode this PR prevents: OpenClaw's safeguard summarizer fires on a claude-cli session that already manages its own compaction, exceeds its budget fighting the backend's own compaction lifecycle, and force-resets the conversation. With `ownsNativeCompaction: true`, the lifecycle returns a no-op and the session continues normally.

The workaround currently deployed (`contextTokens: 1_000_000`) masks the issue by preventing the safeguard from ever firing. This PR fixes the root cause.

## Changes

**`src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts`** -- `ownsNativeCompaction?: boolean` on `CliBackendPlugin`

**`src/agents/cli-backends.ts`** -- Propagated through `ResolvedCliBackend`, `FallbackCliBackendPolicy`, and all 4 resolution paths

**`src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts`** -- Added `resolveCliBackendConfig` to injectable deps. In `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle`, when `ownsNativeCompaction` is true and no native harness endpoint exists, returns a no-op early

**`extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts`** -- `ownsNativeCompaction: true` on claude-cli

## Scope

Only changes behavior for CLI backends that explicitly opt in via `ownsNativeCompaction: true`. Every other backend keeps the existing safeguard. Does not touch the embedded runner path.

## Not tested

- acpx / copilot backends (candidates for future adoption, not wired in this PR)
- Full e2e filling a context window with the fix applied (validated via unit tests covering the exact code path + production failure evidence above)

## Risk / mitigation

- **Risk:** A backend incorrectly declares ownership but does not actually compact -- context grows unbounded. **Mitigation:** Opt-in flag, not default; only set on claude-cli which is known to own compaction.
- **Risk:** Breaks existing codex path. **Mitigation:** `ownsNativeCompaction` early-out only fires when `isNativeHarnessCompactionSession` returns false; codex sessions with `agentHarnessId` still route to the existing native harness path. Verified by test.

## Test plan

- [x] Backend with `ownsNativeCompaction` and no harness endpoint skips compaction (no-op)
- [x] Backend without `ownsNativeCompaction` compacts normally via context engine
- [x] Backend with `ownsNativeCompaction` AND agentHarnessId uses existing native harness path
- [x] `resolveCliBackendConfig("claude-cli")` returns `ownsNativeCompaction: true`
- [x] All 30 existing cli-compaction tests pass (no regressions)
- [x] All 48 existing cli-backends tests pass (no regressions)


---

## Over-budget reproduction & explicit context-cap fix

The defer is proven deterministically on the real code path (see **Test plan**): without the flag OpenClaw compaction runs (`cli-compaction.test.ts:1139`); with the flag and no harness endpoint OpenClaw defers to the backend (`cli-compaction.test.ts:1071`).

```
npx vitest run src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts -t "ownsNativeCompaction"
→ Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 6 passed | 24 skipped (30)
```

A live over-budget capture was initially hard to synthesize because `resolveContextTokensForModel` short-circuited to the GA 1M window **before** consulting a configured value, so an operator could not budget below the native window to exercise the over-budget path. **This PR's second commit fixes that root cause:** an explicit `models.providers.*.contextTokens` **cap** now wins over the GA-1M default (a declared `contextWindow` still does not — GA stays authoritative for the native window). With a low `contextTokens` cap the over-budget branch is reachable for a claude-cli session, where this PR's `ownsNativeCompaction` deferral no-ops it and the session continues via Claude's own compaction — the documented `contextTokens` "budget below the native contextWindow" behavior now works as specified, and claude-cli opts out of OpenClaw compaction like codex's native-harness path.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
